summary: Good‑quality capture of a smoking second set highlighted by a blistering Stranger > Franklin’s run, soaring Eyes of the World and emotional Franklin’s Tower.
review: The consensus is that this second‑set recording sounds clearer than most competing sources, though it suffers from a thin low end and occasional dip in the Estimated Prophet section. Musically the show shines – fans repeatedly point out the over‑the‑top Stranger > Franklin’s jam, a powerful Franklin’s Tower, great Eyes of the World and moving Black Peter moments, making it one of the more memorable Reno shows.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the performance (many 4‑5 star ratings) while noting only modest recording flaws; the weighted blend of strong show quality and decent sound yields a rating above four.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp overall with better clarity than other copies, but missing bottom‑end frequencies and occasional dip in the Estimated Prophet segment; no major drop‑outs reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A high‑energy second set that builds from a spacey Stranger jam into an over‑the‑top Franklin’s Tower, then rolls through strong Eyes of the World and emotional Black Peter; overall pacing is praised as smoking.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note Jerry’s guitar was “letting loose” on Franklin's Tower and the Franklins jam, describing his tone as salty, gritty and full of energy.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work is called "salty gruff" and appreciated despite the recording’s low‑end deficiency.
